question: What functions does the RPV perform, and what components does it support?

nuscale_ch05_0015.title: Reactor Vessel Materials
nuscale_ch05_0015.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module’s reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the core, steam generators, and pressurizer, and serves as a critical barrier to fission product release. The RPV and its components are constructed from materials specified by the ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code to ensure integrity and safety throughout the plant’s design life.

nuscale_ch05_0005.title: System Components
nuscale_ch05_0005.summary: This section describes the main components of the NuScale reactor system, focusing on the Reactor Pressure Vessel (RPV), Reactor Coolant System (RCS) piping, and the Pressurizer (PZR). The RPV serves as a primary barrier to fission product release and houses key reactor internals, while the RCS piping connects various system elements, and the PZR is integrated into the RPV to help control system pressure. Each component's functions and connections are briefly outlined, with references to further detailed sections.

nuscale_ch05_0018.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0018.summary: This section outlines the requirements and procedures for inspection, shipment, installation, operating conditions, inservice surveillance, and threaded fasteners for the NuScale reactor vessel, referencing relevant ASME and ASTM standards. It specifies that the reactor vessel must be shipped and stored under controlled, dry, and pressurized conditions to maintain integrity, and lists the key codes and standards used for compliance.

nuscale_ch05_0014.title: Reactor Vessel
nuscale_ch05_0014.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module (NPM), which integrates the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single reactor pressure vessel (RPV) surrounded by a containment vessel. The RPV serves as a crucial barrier to fission product release, supports various reactor components, and is constructed from materials specified by the ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code to ensure long-term integrity and safety.

edges.description: Section A identifies the RPV as a pressure-retaining vessel that houses key components such as the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer, while Section B describes operational procedures involving these components (SGs, PZR) and their interaction with the RPV during system operations.
Section B references Section A by discussing inspection and handling requirements for the RPV, which is described in detail in Section A as a key component of the reactor coolant system.

Section A describes operational procedures involving the reactor pressure vessel (RPV) and its supported components, while Section B outlines inspection, shipment, and storage requirements for the RPV, connecting through their focus on the RPV's role and handling within the system.

Section B describes the RPV as housing and supporting key components such as the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer, while Section A lists these components and their operational interactions, thus Section B specifies the RPV's support functions for the components detailed in Section A.

Section A references inspection, shipment, and installation requirements for the RPV, while Section B describes the RPV's structural role and the components it supports, thus Section A provides procedural and regulatory context for the physical functions and supported components detailed in Section B.
